sourcePolicy: Installable entries must declare package, skill, source, sourceUrl, license, risk, and lastChecked. Online resolution is verified separately with npm run verify:skills:online.
immutability: Skills CLI installation uses owner/repo plus skill name; this catalog is an allowlist, not a commit-pinned lock. Re-run online verification before release or after changing skill sources.

skills.name: mcp-connectors
skills.reason: Configure and troubleshoot Codex MCP connectors.

skills.name: codex-chef-operator
skills.reason: Bundled local plugin skill for safe Codex Chef repo maintenance, catalog alignment, installer safety, docs updates, and verification gates.

skills.name: git-hygiene
skills.reason: Keep commits, pushes, releases, and artifacts clean.

skills.name: context-budget-planner
skills.reason: Bundled local plugin skill for token/context budgeting, source prioritization, compaction handoff, and verification planning.

skills.name: investigate
skills.reason: Local/global reference. The default installable equivalent is systematic-debugging from obra/superpowers.

skills.name: incident-triage
skills.reason: Triage production regressions and incidents.

skills.name: new-feature
skills.reason: Implement bounded product or code features.

skills.name: refactor-plan
skills.reason: Local/global reference. The default installable equivalent is request-refactor-plan from mattpocock/skills.

skills.name: dependency-upgrade
skills.reason: Upgrade dependencies safely.

skills.name: gh-fix-ci
skills.reason: Debug and fix failed GitHub Actions checks with official OpenAI skill guidance.

skills.name: test-backfill
skills.reason: Add tests around existing behavior.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.reason: Force root-cause investigation before fixes for bugs, test failures, and unexpected behavior.

skills.name: performance-audit
skills.reason: Measure and improve slow paths.

skills.name: db-migration-review
skills.reason: Review and implement schema/migration changes safely.

skills.name: request-refactor-plan
skills.reason: Break broad refactors into small, working, reviewable steps before implementation.

skills.name: release-verify
skills.reason: Verify readiness before release, packaging, and deployment.

skills.name: code-review
skills.reason: Run a correctness-first review pass.

skills.name: sentry-code-review
skills.reason: Perform security/performance-aware reviews.

skills.name: codex-pr-body
skills.reason: Update PR titles and bodies.

skills.name: babysit-pr
skills.reason: Watch PR comments, CI, and mergeability.

skills.name: open-pr
skills.reason: Prepare pull requests when explicitly asked.

skills.name: security-best-practices
skills.reason: Apply framework-specific secure defaults from the official OpenAI skills catalog.

skills.name: security-check
skills.reason: Verify auth, validation, and route security.

skills.name: security-threat-model
skills.reason: Create repository-grounded threat models.

skills.name: frontend-skill
skills.reason: Build visually strong frontend experiences.

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.reason: Test local web apps with Playwright, screenshots, browser logs, and rendered UI evidence.

skills.name: web-quality-audit
skills.reason: Run a general web quality audit across performance, accessibility, SEO, and best practices.

skills.name: seo
skills.reason: Audit crawlability, metadata, structured data, sitemaps, mobile readiness, and search discoverability.

skills.name: accessibility
skills.reason: Review keyboard, focus, forms, ARIA, semantic HTML, and WCAG-oriented UI quality.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.reason: Drive behavior changes through focused tests before implementation.

skills.name: documentation-and-adrs
skills.reason: Create and maintain README, architecture decision records, and durable project documentation.

skills.name: mcp-builder
skills.reason: Design and test high-quality MCP servers with clear tools, schemas, transports, and evaluations.

skills.name: impeccable
skills.reason: Design-polish reference retained for manual install only because it overlaps with the default frontend skill and has shown upstream availability drift.

skills.name: design-taste-frontend
skills.reason: Manual design-taste reference; not installed by default to avoid overlapping frontend/design trigger noise.

skills.name: image-to-code
skills.reason: Manual image-to-code reference; omitted from default install because it is specialized and overlaps with broader frontend workflows.

skills.name: high-end-visual-design
skills.reason: Manual visual-design reference; omitted from default install to keep the starter focused and reduce duplicate design triggers.

skills.name: web-design-guidelines
skills.reason: Manual Vercel design-guidelines reference; replaced in default install by broader web-quality and accessibility skills.

skills.name: vercel-react-best-practices
skills.reason: Manual React/Next.js reference; not installed by default because the starter should remain framework-neutral.

skills.name: vercel-optimize
skills.reason: Manual Vercel hosting reference; not installed by default because authenticated/vendor hosting work should stay opt-in.

skills.name: context-map
skills.reason: Manual context-engineering reference retained for users who want extra context mapping patterns beyond the bundled context architect and context budget planner.

skills.name: ai-project-starter
skills.reason: First-party Codex Chef ecosystem skill for AI-coding-ready project context, starter docs, agent instruction files, enterprise starter docs, and vibe-coding guardrails. Included in -All because it extends the bundled context_architect and context-budget-planner without requiring credentials.

skills.name: what-context-needed
skills.reason: Manual context-selection reference; not default because Codex Chef already ships context_architect plus a local context-budget-planner.

skills.name: prompt-architect
skills.reason: First-party Codex Chef ecosystem skill for plan-first, approval-gated, security-aware Codex prompt generation and prompt audits. Included in -All because it complements the bundled prompt_architect with reusable prompt workflows.

skills.name: prompt-engineering-patterns
skills.reason: Manual prompt-pattern reference; not default because Codex Chef already includes prompt_architect and avoids duplicate prompt-skill triggers.

skills.name: ai-prompt-engineering-safety-review
skills.reason: Manual prompt-security reference for teams that want an extra review workflow without making it a noisy default trigger.

skills.name: ai-skill-create
skills.reason: First-party Codex Chef ecosystem skill for creating, improving, validating, forward-testing, and packaging Codex skills and plugins. Included in -All because Codex Chef should ship a complete skill-authoring path while keeping auth and publishing actions approval-gated.

skills.name: memory-safety-patterns
skills.reason: Manual memory-safety reference only; memory workflows can expose private local context and should stay opt-in.

skills.name: vercel-cli-with-tokens
skills.reason: Manual Vercel auth-token reference. This is for deployment tokens, not LLM context budgeting, so authenticated usage must stay opt-in.

skills.name: playwright
skills.reason: Local/global reference. The default installable equivalent is webapp-testing from anthropics/skills.

skills.name: offline-diagram-triplet
skills.reason: Bundled local plugin skill that converts Mermaid source into editable Excalidraw plus SVG/PNG artifacts with zero network.
